Fixed by:\n- Using `os.tmpdir()` + `fs.mkdtempSync` for test directories\n- Injecting temp dirs into ProblemManager, ProblemIndexer, ProblemExtractor, ReviewPipeline constructors (all already accepted injectable dirs)\n- Added `cleanup()` method, called in `runAll()`\n- Changed test2/test3 from `require()` (which had side effects) to `node --check` syntax check\n7/7 passing, zero Vault side effects verified.\n\n### C3: Phase 15 and 16 validators\nCreated `validate-phase-15.js`:\n- 6 tests: scripts exist, syntax checks (node --check), Vault structure, session summaries, Known-Problems casing\n- `Vault/07-Decisions/ADR-INFRA-003.md` — documents the chromadb JS SDK decision:\n- Context: v1 API removed, v2 routes broken\n- Options: JS SDK (chosen), fix raw HTTP, replace Chroma\n- Decision: chromadb JS SDK\n- Follow-up actions listed\nUpdated DECISIONS.md index with \"Decision 10: Chroma Client Strategy → ADR-INFRA-003\"\n\nAll C1-C5 committed as `5c531fd`, pushed.\n\n## Phase 16: Chroma Pipeline Rebuild\n\n### Setup\n- Started Chroma Docker container: `docker compose up -d chroma`\n- Installed chromadb SDK: `npm install chromadb` → added as `\"chromadb\": \"^3.4.3\"` dependency\n- Tested SDK: `ChromaClient({host:'localhost', port:8000})` works\n- Needed embeddings: installed `@chroma-core/default-embed`\n- Tested full SDK cycle (create, add, query, delete) — works\n\n### chroma-ingest.js rewrite\nComplete rewrite using chromadb JS SDK:\n- Constructor: `new ChromaClient({ host, port })` + `new DefaultEmbeddingFunction()`\n- Collections via `client.getOrCreateCollection({ name, embeddingFunction: ef })`\n- Documents via `col.upsert({ ids, documents, metadatas })` — idempotent\n- Classification fixes needed:\n  1. Added `complete` and `verified` to facts allowlist (phase docs use status:complete)\n  2. Standards check moved BEFORE authority check (standards have authority:facts too)\n  3. `is_authoritative` set true for both facts AND standards keys\n- Exports: `{ buildMetadata, deriveDocumentType, deriveDomain, classifyDocument, parseYamlFrontmatter }`\n\n### context-assembly.js rewrite\nComplete rewrite using chromadb JS SDK:\n- `getClient()` / `getEf()` lazy init functions\n- `queryCollection(client, ef, name, query, nResults, filters)` — gracefully returns [] if collection doesn't exist\n- Standards queried WITHOUT `is_authoritative` filter (just query the collection directly)\n- Local Vault sources unchanged: `loadAgentMemory`, `loadRelationships`, `loadSessionHandoff`\n- Graceful degradation if Chroma unavailable (local sources still load)\n- Exports: `{ assembleContext, queryCollection }`\n\n### Re-index results\nAfter all fixes, final ingest result:\n- Facts: 66 documents\n- Sessions: 76 documents  \n- Standards: 4 documents\n- Total ingested: 146\n- Rejected: 2 (both `Templates/*.md` with `authority:facts` + `status:draft` — correct)\n- Errors: 0\n\n### Context assembly verification\nQuery \"design a database layer\" returned:\n- Standards: 4 (Architecture, Coding, Documentation, Security Standards)\n- Facts: 5\n- Relationships: 3\n- Session handoff: 2026-06-10\n- Total: 12 items\n- Summary: \"Context assembled: Standards: 4, Facts: 5, Relationships: 3, Handoff: 2026-06-10\"\n\n## Pending work at compaction\nPhase 16 is functionally complete (pipeline working). Still needed:\n1. Update validate-phase-16.js to convert warnings to passes (Chroma is now up, ADR exists)\n2. Add `npm run ingest` script to package.json\n3. Commit and push Phase 16\n4. Update STATUS.md to mark Phase 16 complete\n\nThe todo list at time of compaction:\n1. ✅ Rewrite chroma-ingest.js\n2. ✅ Rewrite context-assembly.js\n3. ✅ Run full Vault re-index\n4. pending: Update validate-phase-16.js — warnings become passes\n5. pending: Commit and push Phase 16\n</analysis>\n\n<summary>\n1. Primary Request and Intent:\n   The session had two main arcs:\n   \n   **Arc 1 — Project Discovery Interview + Spec:** User invoked `/discover` for a structured interview. The interview established the vision for the framework: end-to-end pipeline (interview → research → recommend → build → review → ship) for building any type of project with AI. Key decisions: agents write all tests but present test plan to human first; projects live in `Projects/[category]/[name]/`; framework is copied (not referenced) at scaffold time; project Vaults connect to framework via Chroma cross-indexing; soft budget ceiling per project; category folders created on first use. Full BR/FR/NFR spec written to `Vault/09-Requirements/Project Build Pipeline/` (19 requirements). Phase 18 planned with 6 implementation stages.\n   \n   **Arc 2 — Phase 17 Cleanup (C1-C5) + Phase 16 Chroma Rebuild:** After the spec was committed, all 5 Phase 17 cleanup items were completed, then Phase 16 was implemented (Chroma pipeline rebuilt using chromadb JS SDK).\n\n2. Uses `ChromaClient({ host, port })` + `DefaultEmbeddingFunction()`\n   - `client.getOrCreateCollection({ name, embeddingFunction: ef })`\n   - `col.upsert({ ids, documents, metadatas })` — idempotent\n   - Classification order: standards first, then facts, then sessions, then default sessions\n   - Facts allowlist: `['accepted', 'approved', 'current', 'active', 'complete', 'verified']`\n   - `is_authoritative: classification.key === 'facts' || classification.key === 'standards'`\n   - Lazy client init: `getClient()` / `getEf()`\n   - `queryCollection(client, ef, name, query, nResults, filters)` — returns `[]` if collection missing\n   - Standards queried WITHOUT `is_authoritative` filter (just query collection directly)\n   - Facts queried WITH `{ is_authoritative: true }` filter\n   - Graceful degradation: Chroma errors caught, local sources (agent memory, relationships, handoff) still loaded\n   - Exports: `{ assembleContext, queryCollection }`\n\n4. Errors and fixes:\n   - **`validate-phase-11.js` writing to production Vault:** `require()` of scripts with side effects + ProblemManager defaulting to Vault path. Fixed with temp dir injection and `node --check` syntax validation.\n   - **Known-Problem file `Problem-infra-chroma-ingestion-api-incompatibility.md` had `type: KnownProblem`:** Fixed to `type: knownproblem` (lowercase).\n   - **`seed-agent-memory.js` and `session-handoff.js` running on `require()`:** Both execute immediately when required. Fixed by using `execSync('node --check ...')` instead.\n   - **Standards collection ingesting 0 documents:** Standards files have `authority: facts` AND `type: Standard`. The authority branch matched first, routing them to facts. Fixed by checking `type === 'Standard'` BEFORE the authority check.\n   - **Standards not returned by context assembly (0 results):** Standards metadata had `is_authoritative: false` because they route via the `standards` key, not `facts`. Fixed by setting `is_authoritative: true` for both `key === 'facts'` and `key === 'standards'`. Also removed the `is_authoritative: true` filter from the global-standards query (just query the collection directly).\n   - **chromadb SDK missing embedding function:** `@chroma-core/default-embed` package not installed. Fixed with `npm install @chroma-core/default-embed`.\n   - **`ChromaClient` deprecated `path` argument:** Changed to `new ChromaClient({ host: 'localhost', port: 8000 })`.\n\n5. Problem Solving:\n   - **Phase 16 Chroma pipeline:** Root cause was v1 HTTP API removal + invalid v2 routes. Solution: chromadb JS SDK abstracts HTTP completely. Full rewrite of both files. After fixes: 146/148 documents ingested (2 correctly rejected as draft), all 3 collections populated (66 facts, 76 sessions, 4 standards). Context assembly verified working with query returning 12 items including all 4 standards.\n   - **Test isolation:** All 4 Phase 11 test classes accept injectable directories — solution was to inject temp dirs rather than patching the classes themselves.\n   - **Ingestion idempotency:** Used `col.upsert()` instead of `col.add()` so re-runs don't fail on duplicate IDs.\n\n6. All user messages:\n   - \"This session is being continued from a previous conversation...\" [context injection — not user message]\n   - \"The problem I am trying to solve is insufficient frameworks for AI agents to utilize when doing complex tasks like building an app or a tool or a game. I want to make it as easy as possible to have the tools needed to make anything with the help of ai and ai agents. could you guage that from looking at the project head on?\"\n   - \"The primary user of the framework is me and claude and in the future, ai agents running autonomously, but it should be readable and usable by both humans and ai agents. im imagining a single developer experience, but to make it as efficient and seamless as possible.\"\n   - \"if we were making an app for instance, we would need to figure out the tech stack, the user base, if we are running it locally, what dependencies are being used, if it should be online or a desktop or phone app, are there any frameworks that would help, what tools would make this app better at what its trying to accomplish? making something new should be asking all these questions and finding out al of this information to make a fully functioning proper app. I want this project to help with that process and make it seamless for humans and for AI. I want to make it easy and accessible to build anything with the help of ai\"\n   - \"all three in sequence\"\n   - \"I want to set a plan at the beginning, have it run autonomously until its done running tests and verifying evrything works properly and then getting a rundown of what changed before making the commits and pushes to github\"\n   - \"it should use the tools in its disposal to fix it and if it cant find an immediate solution, let it do a quick websearch of the problem and if it cant find an immediate solution there, bring it to my attention and notate it in the problems page we have\"\n   - \"it should built a file structure of close to what the final project should look like. and then it create a multi step plan or phases that allow the project to be broken down in manageable sections such as security, or gui, or frontend/backend, or something that makes sense. I will leave bits like that up to ai recommendations and change if need be.\"\n   - \"Something deployable\"\n   - \"there are universal rules for all ai agents found in claude.md and the .claude folder and more to be added, but for projects, we can come up with a rulebook for each project when we are doing the initial interview for each project.\"\n   - \"looks good, is there anything you would add or change?\"\n   - \"yes, add your suggestions and let me know if you have any more\"\n   - \"there should be a soft budget that if planning to go over should be conferred with human about. projects should in a subfolder called projects and then with subfolder in them with sections for things like games, tools, desktop apps and such. I want this structure because I want all future projects to have all the tools that the framwork has to offer including the vault. I can then make a new repo on github and make add the project there. what do you think?\"\n   - \"im ok with your recommendation. lets continue with that instead of my approach. I would prefer the project to copy the framework tools and if changes need to be made to the copied tools to better fit the project then so be it. What I really want though, is that seperate project vault to be connected to the framework vault so future projects can reference it when being built. I do not have a starter list of folders. or a starter anything for that matter, everything is being build from scratch either now or when a project is being made\"\n   - \"Tests are made by the agents, but the purpose for the tests and what the tests test should be told to human. commit everything after you make the necessary changes and then add phase 18 to the roadmap so we can start on phase 17\"\n   - \"ready\" [start Phase 17]\n   - \"continue with phase 16\"\n\n7. Fixed with temp dir injection and `node --check` syntax validation.\n   - **Known-Problem file `Problem-infra-chroma-ingestion-api-incompatibility.md` had `type: KnownProblem`:** Fixed to `type: knownproblem` (lowercase).\n   - **`seed-agent-memory.js` and `session-handoff.js` running on `require()`:** Both execute immediately when required. Fixed by using `execSync('node --check ...')` instead.\n   - **Standards collection ingesting 0 documents:** Standards files have `authority: facts` AND `type: Standard`. The authority branch matched first, routing them to facts. Fixed by checking `type === 'Standard'` BEFORE the authority check.\n   - **Standards not returned by context assembly (0 results):** Standards metadata had `is_authoritative: false` because they route via the `standards` key, not `facts`. Fixed by setting `is_authoritative: true` for both `key === 'facts'` and `key === 'standards'`. Also removed the `is_authoritative: true` filter from the global-standards query (just query the collection directly).\n   - **chromadb SDK missing embedding function:** `@chroma-core/default-embed` package not installed. Fixed with `npm install @chroma-core/default-embed`.\n   - **`ChromaClient` deprecated `path` argument:** Changed to `new ChromaClient({ host: 'localhost', port: 8000 })`.\n\n5. Problem Solving:\n   - **Phase 16 Chroma pipeline:** Root cause was v1 HTTP API removal + invalid v2 routes. Solution: chromadb JS SDK abstracts HTTP completely. Full rewrite of both files. After fixes: 146/148 documents ingested (2 correctly rejected as draft), all 3 collections populated (66 facts, 76 sessions, 4 standards). Context assembly verified working with query returning 12 items including all 4 standards.\n   - **Test isolation:** All 4 Phase 11 test classes accept injectable directories — solution was to inject temp dirs rather than patching the classes themselves.\n   - **Ingestion idempotency:** Used `col.upsert()` instead of `col.add()` so re-runs don't fail on duplicate IDs.\n\n6. All user messages:\n   - \"This session is being continued from a previous conversation...\" [context injection — not user message]\n   - \"The problem I am trying to solve is insufficient frameworks for AI agents to utilize when doing complex tasks like building an app or a tool or a game. I want to make it as easy as possible to have the tools needed to make anything with the help of ai and ai agents. could you guage that from looking at the project head on?\"\n   - \"The primary user of the framework is me and claude and in the future, ai agents running autonomously, but it should be readable and usable by both humans and ai agents. im imagining a single developer experience, but to make it as efficient and seamless as possible.\"\n   - \"if we were making an app for instance, we would need to figure out the tech stack, the user base, if we are running it locally, what dependencies are being used, if it should be online or a desktop or phone app, are there any frameworks that would help, what tools would make this app better at what its trying to accomplish? making something new should be asking all these questions and finding out al of this information to make a fully functioning proper app. I want this project to help with that process and make it seamless for humans and for AI. I want to make it easy and accessible to build anything with the help of ai\"\n   - \"all three in sequence\"\n   - \"I want to set a plan at the beginning, have it run autonomously until its done running tests and verifying evrything works properly and then getting a rundown of what changed before making the commits and pushes to github\"\n   - \"it should use the tools in its disposal to fix it and if it cant find an immediate solution, let it do a quick websearch of the problem and if it cant find an immediate solution there, bring it to my attention and notate it in the problems page we have\"\n   - \"it should built a file structure of close to what the final project should look like. and then it create a multi step plan or phases that allow the project to be broken down in manageable sections such as security, or gui, or frontend/backend, or something that makes sense. I will leave bits like that up to ai recommendations and change if need be.\"\n   - \"Something deployable\"\n   - \"there are universal rules for all ai agents found in claude.md and the .claude folder and more to be added, but for projects, we can come up with a rulebook for each project when we are doing the initial interview for each project.\"\n   - \"looks good, is there anything you would add or change?\"\n   - \"yes, add your suggestions and let me know if you have any more\"\n   - \"there should be a soft budget that if planning to go over should be conferred with human about. projects should in a subfolder called projects and then with subfolder in them with sections for things like games, tools, desktop apps and such. I want this structure because I want all future projects to have all the tools that the framwork has to offer including the vault. I can then make a new repo on github and make add the project there. what do you think?\"\n   - \"im ok with your recommendation. lets continue with that instead of my approach. I would prefer the project to copy the framework tools and if changes need to be made to the copied tools to better fit the project then so be it. What I really want though, is that seperate project vault to be connected to the framework vault so future projects can reference it when being built. I do not have a starter list of folders. or a starter anything for that matter, everything is being build from scratch either now or when a project is being made\"\n   - \"Tests are made by the agents, but the purpose for the tests and what the tests test should be told to human. commit everything after you make the necessary changes and then add phase 18 to the roadmap so we can start on phase 17\"\n   - \"ready\" [start Phase 17]\n   - \"continue with phase 16\"\n\n7. Pending Tasks:\n   - **Update `validate-phase-16.js`** — Chroma is now running and ADR-INFRA-003 exists; the 2 warnings should now be passes. ## Decisions Made

- \n- Context: v1 API removed, v2 routes broken\n- Options: JS SDK (chosen), fix raw HTTP, replace Chroma\n- Decision: chromadb JS SDK\n- Follow-up actions listed\nUpdated DECISIONS.md index with \"Decision 10: Chroma Client Strategy → ADR-INFRA-003\"\n\nAll C1-C5 committed as `5c531fd`, pushed.\n\n## Phase 16: Chroma Pipeline Rebuild\n\n### Setup\n- Started Chroma Docker container: `docker compose up -d chroma`\n- Installed chromadb SDK: `npm install chromadb` → added as `\"chromadb\": \"^3.4.3\"` dependency\n- Tested SDK: `ChromaClient({host:'localhost', port:8000})` works\n- Needed embeddings: installed `@chroma-core/default-embed`\n- Tested full SDK cycle (create, add, query, delete) — works\n\n### chroma-ingest.js rewrite\nComplete rewrite using chromadb JS SDK:\n- Constructor: `new ChromaClient({ host, port })` + `new DefaultEmbeddingFunction()`\n- Collections via `client.getOrCreateCollection({ name, embeddingFunction: ef })`\n- Documents via `col.upsert({ ids, documents, metadatas })` — idempotent\n- Classification fixes needed:\n  1. Added `complete` and `verified` to facts allowlist (phase docs use status:complete)\n  2. Standards check moved BEFORE authority check (standards have authority:facts too)\n  3. `is_authoritative` set true for both facts AND standards keys\n- Exports: `{ buildMetadata, deriveDocumentType, deriveDomain, classifyDocument, parseYamlFrontmatter }`\n\n### context-assembly.js rewrite\nComplete rewrite using chromadb JS SDK:\n- `getClient()` / `getEf()` lazy init functions\n- `queryCollection(client, ef, name, query, nResults, filters)` — gracefully returns [] if collection doesn't exist\n- Standards queried WITHOUT `is_authoritative` filter (just query the collection directly)\n- Local Vault sources unchanged: `loadAgentMemory`, `loadRelationships`, `loadSessionHandoff`\n- Graceful degradation if Chroma unavailable (local sources still load)\n- Exports: `{ assembleContext, queryCollection }`\n\n### Re-index results\nAfter all fixes, final ingest result:\n- Facts: 66 documents\n- Sessions: 76 documents  \n- Standards: 4 documents\n- Total ingested: 146\n- Rejected: 2 (both `Templates/*.md` with `authority:facts` + `status:draft` — correct)\n- Errors: 0\n\n### Context assembly verification\nQuery \"design a database layer\" returned:\n- Standards: 4 (Architecture, Coding, Documentation, Security Standards)\n- Facts: 5\n- Relationships: 3\n- Session handoff: 2026-06-10\n- Total: 12 items\n- Summary: \"Context assembled: Standards: 4, Facts: 5, Relationships: 3, Handoff: 2026-06-10\"\n\n## Pending work at compaction\nPhase 16 is functionally complete (pipeline working). Still needed:\n1. Update validate-phase-16.js to convert warnings to passes (Chroma is now up, ADR exists)\n2. Add `npm run ingest` script to package.json\n3. Commit and push Phase 16\n4. Update STATUS.md to mark Phase 16 complete\n\nThe todo list at time of compaction:\n1. ✅ Rewrite chroma-ingest.js\n2. ✅ Rewrite context-assembly.js\n3. ✅ Run full Vault re-index\n4. pending: Update validate-phase-16.js — warnings become passes\n5. pending: Commit and push Phase 16\n</analysis>\n\n<summary>\n1. Primary Request and Intent:\n   The session had two main arcs:\n   \n   **Arc 1 — Project Discovery Interview + Spec:** User invoked `/discover` for a structured interview. The interview established the vision for the framework: end-to-end pipeline (interview → research → recommend → build → review → ship) for building any type of project with AI. Key decisions: agents write all tests but present test plan to human first; projects live in `Projects/[category]/[name]/`; framework is copied (not referenced) at scaffold time; project Vaults connect to framework via Chroma cross-indexing; soft budget ceiling per project; category folders created on first use. Full BR/FR/NFR spec written to `Vault/09-Requirements/Project Build Pipeline/` (19 requirements). Phase 18 planned with 6 implementation stages.\n   \n   **Arc 2 — Phase 17 Cleanup (C1-C5) + Phase 16 Chroma Rebuild:** After the spec was committed, all 5 Phase 17 cleanup items were completed, then Phase 16 was implemented (Chroma pipeline rebuilt using chromadb JS SDK).\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n   - Project Build Pipeline: interview → research → recommend → build → review → ship\n   - Agents write all tests; present test plan summary to human before writing\n   - `Projects/[category]/[name]/` structure; framework copied at scaffold; Chroma cross-indexing for reusability\n   - Soft budget ceiling per project; agent alerts if approaching ceiling\n   - chromadb JS SDK (`chromadb` + `@chroma-core/default-embed`) replacing broken v1/v2 HTTP calls\n   - `ChromaClient({ host, port })` + `DefaultEmbeddingFunction()` constructor pattern\n   - `col.upsert()` for idempotent ingestion\n   - Classification order: standards checked BEFORE authority (standards have authority:facts too)\n   - `is_authoritative: true` for both facts AND standards collections\n   - `node --check` for syntax-only validation (no side effects vs `require()`)\n   - `os.tmpdir()` + `fs.mkdtempSync()` for test isolation\n   - Phase 16 validator uses warnings (not failures) for Chroma/ADR checks during rebuild\n\n3. Files and Code Sections:\n\n   **`Vault/09-Requirements/Project Build Pipeline/Business Requirements.md`** (NEW)\n   - 5 BRs: Seamless E2E creation, Human authority at commit boundaries, Universal+project governance, Knowledge compounds across projects, Human+AI readable\n   \n   **`Vault/09-Requirements/Project Build Pipeline/Functional Requirements.md`** (NEW)\n   - 12 FRs covering all 6 pipeline stages. Key: FR-PBP-009 test authorship resolved (agents write, present plan to human first), FR-PBP-010 diff summary + decision log\n   \n   **`Vault/09-Requirements/Project Build Pipeline/Non-Functional Requirements.md`** (NEW)\n   - 7 NFRs. Key: NFR-PBP-004 soft budget ceiling; NFR-PBP-005 project location resolved (`Projects/[category]/[name]/`, Chroma cross-indexing, Registry.md); NFR-PBP-006 Guardian monitoring optional post-ship\n   \n   **`Vault/03-Projects/AI Software Factory/Phase-14-17-Roadmap.md`** (UPDATED)\n   - Extended to Phase 18 with 6 stages: 18.1 scaffold script, 18.2 discovery skill update, 18.3 Chroma cross-project indexing, 18.4 phase plan generator, 18.5 autonomous build loop, 18.6 review and ship\n   \n   **`.claude/scripts/validate-phase-11.js`** (UPDATED)\n   - Added temp dir isolation to prevent Vault side effects:\n   ```javascript\n   constructor() {\n     this.testDir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'phase11-test-'));\n     this.testProblemsDir = path.join(this.testDir, 'known-problems');\n     this.testReviewsDir = path.join(this.testDir, 'reviews');\n     fs.mkdirSync(this.testProblemsDir, { recursive: true });\n     fs.mkdirSync(this.testReviewsDir, { recursive: true });\n     this.extractor = new ProblemExtractor(this.testReviewsDir);\n     this.manager = new ProblemManager(this.testProblemsDir);\n     this.indexer = new ProblemIndexer(this.testProblemsDir);\n     this.pipeline = new ReviewPipeline(this.testReviewsDir);\n   }\n   cleanup() { fs.rmSync(this.testDir, { recursive: true, force: true }); }\n   ```\n   - Test 2/3 changed from `require()` to `node --check` syntax check via `execSync`\n   \n   **`.claude/scripts/validate-phase-15.js`** (NEW)\n   - 6 tests: scripts exist, seed-agent-memory.js syntax, session-handoff.js syntax, Vault directory structure, session summaries present, Known-Problems frontmatter casing\n   \n   **`.claude/scripts/validate-phase-16.js`** (NEW)\n   - 5 checks: pipeline scripts exist, chroma-ingest.js syntax, context-assembly.js syntax, Chroma connectivity (warn not fail), ADR-INFRA-003 exists (warn not fail)\n   - Uses `pass/fail/warn` triad — warnings don't cause non-zero exit\n   \n   **`package.json`** (UPDATED)\n   - Added `test:phase-15`, `test:phase-16`\n   - Extended `test:all` to include both\n   - Added `chromadb: \"^3.4.3\"` and `@chroma-core/default-embed` dependencies\n   \n   **`Vault/07-Decisions/ADR-INFRA-003.md`** (NEW)\n   - Documents chromadb JS SDK decision: v1 removed, v2 routes broken, Option A (JS SDK) chosen\n   - Follow-up actions: install chromadb, rewrite ingest + assembly, re-index, update validator\n   \n   **`Vault/03-Projects/AI Software Factory/Context-Assembly-API.md`** (NEW — moved from scripts)\n   - Phase 5 context assembly API reference doc, proper Vault frontmatter added\n   \n   **`Vault/03-Projects/AI Software Factory/Phase-14-Integration-Guide.md`** (NEW — moved from scripts)\n   - Phase 14 FSM/VaultValidator/MCPWhitelister integration guide, proper Vault frontmatter\n   \n   **`.claude/scripts/chroma-ingest.js`** (FULL REWRITE)\n   - Uses `ChromaClient({ host, port })` + `DefaultEmbeddingFunction()`\n   - `client.getOrCreateCollection({ name, embeddingFunction: ef })`\n   - `col.upsert({ ids, documents, metadatas })` — idempotent\n   - Classification order: standards first, then facts, then sessions, then default sessions\n   - Facts allowlist: `['accepted', 'approved', 'current', 'active', 'complete', 'verified']`\n   - `is_authoritative: classification.key === 'facts' || classification.key === 'standards'`\n   - Standards detected by: `type === 'standard' || frontmatter.type === 'Standard'`\n   - Exports: `{ buildMetadata, deriveDocumentType, deriveDomain, classifyDocument, parseYamlFrontmatter }`\n   \n   **`.claude/scripts/context-assembly.js`** (FULL REWRITE)\n   - Lazy client init: `getClient()` / `getEf()`\n   - `queryCollection(client, ef, name, query, nResults, filters)` — returns `[]` if collection missing\n   - Standards queried WITHOUT `is_authoritative` filter (just query collection directly)\n   - Facts queried WITH `{ is_authoritative: true }` filter\n   - Graceful degradation: Chroma errors caught, local sources (agent memory, relationships, handoff) still loaded\n   - Exports: `{ assembleContext, queryCollection }`\n\n4.
